title: Undeterred by Channel’s Perils, Desperate Migrants Still Plan to Cross
content: The number of migrants setting off into the English Channel by boat has soared in recent months. The deaths Wednesday of at least 27 people trying to make the crossing illustrate how dangerous it is.

more_info.byline: By Constant Méheut and Norimitsu Onishi
des_facet: Middle East and Africa Migrant Crisis

en_title: Undeterred by Channel’s Perils, Desperate Migrants Still Plan to Cross
en_content: The number of migrants setting off into the English Channel by boat has soared in recent months. The deaths Wednesday of at least 27 people trying to make the crossing illustrate how dangerous it is.

title: Coffee or Chai? At 2 Kolkata Cafes, ‘Adda’ Is What’s Really on the Menu
content: Unrestricted talk, or “adda,” is the main draw at two storied cafes that are at once museums to the city’s intellectual history — and places for debate.
en_title: Coffee or Chai? At 2 Kolkata Cafes, ‘Adda’ Is What’s Really on the Menu
en_content: Unrestricted talk, or “adda,” is the main draw at two storied cafes that are at once museums to the city’s intellectual history — and places for debate.

title: ‘Today’s Outfit Is Confidence’: Atypical TikTok Star Enchants Mexico
content: Middle-aged and scarred, a Mexican journalist is breaking the mold of social media influencers with a message of pure positivity that resonates in dark times.
en_title: ‘Today’s Outfit Is Confidence’: Atypical TikTok Star Enchants Mexico

title: Where Afghanistan’s New Taliban Leaders Went to School
content: Darul Uloom Haqqania in Pakistan argues that the madrasa and its graduates have changed. Some worry they could be the source of new radicalism.
en_title: Where Afghanistan’s New Taliban Leaders Went to School

title: Peng Shuai’s Accusation Pierced the Privileged Citadel of Chinese Politics
content: Zhang Gaoli was best known as a low-key technocrat. Then a Chinese tennis star’s allegations made him a symbol of a system that bristles against scrutiny.

more_info.byline: By Chris Buckley and Steven Lee Myers

en_title: Peng Shuai’s Accusation Pierced the Privileged Citadel of Chinese Politics
en_content: Zhang Gaoli was best known as a low-key technocrat. Then a Chinese tennis star’s allegations made him a symbol of a system that bristles against scrutiny.

title: Russian Mine Blast Kills Dozens, Among Them Rescuers
content: Investigators said a gas buildup at a Siberian mine with a history of problems led to the deaths of at least 52 people.
en_title: Russian Mine Blast Kills Dozens, Among Them Rescuers
en_content: Investigators said a gas buildup at a Siberian mine with a history of problems led to the deaths of at least 52 people.

title: Protests Rock Solomon Islands: Here’s What’s Behind the Unrest
content: Protesters tried to storm the prime minister’s residence, and he blamed the central government’s 2019 decision to switch allegiances to Beijing from Taipei for the violence.
en_title: Protests Rock Solomon Islands: Here’s What’s Behind the Unrest
en_content: Protesters tried to storm the prime minister’s residence, and he blamed the central government’s 2019 decision to switch allegiances to Beijing from Taipei for the violence.

title: Sweden Finally Chose a Prime Minister. She Lasted About 7 Hours.
content: Magdalena Andersson, Sweden’s first female prime minister, quit after her government’s budget was defeated on her first day in office and her coalition partners bolted.
en_title: Sweden Finally Chose a Prime Minister. She Lasted About 7 Hours.
en_content: Magdalena Andersson, Sweden’s first female prime minister, quit after her government’s budget was defeated on her first day in office and her coalition partners bolted.

title: Europe is being tested by waves of cases that threaten its progress against the pandemic.
content: The deteriorating situation is also worrisome for the United States, as trends in new Covid cases tended to follow Europe by weeks.

title: Travel restrictions multiply for African countries over fears of a new variant.

title: Will Germany’s ‘Debt Brake’ Stop Its Green Ambitions?
content: The new government has big plans to modernize the economy and switch to renewable energy. But the question remains how it will pay for it.

title: A native New Yorker wrangles a balloon in the Thanksgiving parade.
content: Decked in a white jumpsuit and blue smock with a Pikachu cartoon on it, George Gustines maneuvered the huge blowup cartoon character down New York’s streets on Thursday.

title: The Macy’s parade brings customers back for turkey hats and coffee.
content: Business was brisk for some as the Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade returned to New York City. Delis, supermarkets, and even a man selling a turkey hat were overjoyed by the thousands of spectators.

content: About 2.3 million people passed through security at airports on Wednesday, more than twice as many as in 2020.

title: How one man realized his dream of becoming a balloon handler in the parade.

title: The French rush to get booster shots as the government eyes more restrictions.
content: The French health minister said the latest wave of cases would be worse than the one during the summer. Adults will need a booster for their health pass to remain valid.
